visa_types.name: Visiting Family/Friend
visa_types.documents_required:
Document Checklist – Click here
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[svk > gbr > en > Table:Visiting Family/Friend Visa > Visa Fees 1]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in Cash/QR or by Debit/Credit Card. AmEx not accepted.
VFS Service charge:
Applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 45 Euros (Inclusive VAT 20%) payable in cash or by Debit/Credit Card.
Exemptions apply to:
Citizens of Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia: 35 Euros
Citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Moldova, Georgia, Ukraine: 30 Euros
Please Note:
- The child between 0 - 12 years on the date of submission at VFS are Biometric exempt.
- The applicable visa fee in GBP is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.
visa_types.processing_time:
From the date of receipt of application at the Embassy of Slovakia, it takes 15 working days to process the visa application. The duration of days when the application is in transit between the Visa Application Centre and the Embassy are not counted. 
In individual cases, where further scrutiny of the application is necessary, the deadline may be extended up to a maximum of 45 calendar days after the application has been considered admissible.
visa_types.forms:
Please fill your application form in all fields by using the following link:
Slovakia Online Visa Application Form (https://ezov.mzv.sk/e-zov/)
Please make sure:
- Place of Birth: fill with the city you were born
- Applicant’s home address and email address: type full address, including postcode and city, and a valid email address.
- Residence in a country other than the country of current nationality: enter your share code for immigration status
- Entry permit for the final country of destination, where applicable: leave this field blank
Application must be signed with blue-ink pen only
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Visit to family or friend visa application is applicable in case you are travelling for visiting your family member or friend.
Applicants will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Visit to family or friend visa application is applicable in case you are travelling for visiting your family member or friend.
Applicants will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.

## Visa Fees

[svk > gbr > en > Table:Visiting Family/Friend Visa > Visa Fees 1]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in Cash/QR or by Debit/Credit Card. AmEx not accepted.
VFS Service charge:
Applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 45 Euros (Inclusive VAT 20%) payable in cash or by Debit/Credit Card.
Exemptions apply to:
Citizens of Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia: 35 Euros
Citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Moldova, Georgia, Ukraine: 30 Euros
Please Note:
- The child between 0 - 12 years on the date of submission at VFS are Biometric exempt.
- The applicable visa fee in GBP is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

## Documents Required
Document Checklist – Click here
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries.

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent colour photograph (not more than 6 months old) of yourself, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Please stick the photograph on the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements (click here), then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application centre. 

## Processing Time
From the date of receipt of application at the Embassy of Slovakia, it takes 15 working days to process the visa application. The duration of days when the application is in transit between the Visa Application Centre and the Embassy are not counted. 
In individual cases, where further scrutiny of the application is necessary, the deadline may be extended up to a maximum of 45 calendar days after the application has been considered admissible.

## Download Forms
Please fill your application form in all fields by using the following link:
Slovakia Online Visa Application Form (https://ezov.mzv.sk/e-zov/)
Please make sure:
- Place of Birth: fill with the city you were born
- Applicant’s home address and email address: type full address, including postcode and city, and a valid email address.
- Residence in a country other than the country of current nationality: enter your share code for immigration status
- Entry permit for the final country of destination, where applicable: leave this field blank
Application must be signed with blue-ink pen only

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Notice for all visa applicants 
Application for a visa must be submitted in person at the visa centre. All applicants are asked to arrange their appointment in advance. 
Exemptions apply to nationals of some countries which have signed Community-level visa facilitation agreements. Such countries include Albania, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Belarus, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Cape Verde, Montenegro, Macedonia, Moldova, the Russian Federation, Serbia, Georgia, Turkey and Ukraine. With respect to the nationals of the countries, the decision must be delivered within 10 calendar days. Nationals of Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Georgia, Macedonia, Montenegro, Serbia and Ukraine don´t need a visa, if they are holders of biometric passports. 
The submission of complete documentation does not in any way guarantee that your application will be approved. 
In individual cases, where further scrutiny of the application is necessary, the deadline may be extended up to a maximum of 45 calendar days after the application has been considered admissible.
Citizen of Iran, Morocco, Vietnam, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Belarus must submit Slovak Foreign Police verified invitation: Pozvanie cudzinca, Ministerstvo vnútra Slovenskej republiky (https://www.minv.sk/?pozvanie-cudzinca)
The United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland is not a member of the Schengen Area. Citizens of the European Economic Area (EEA) do not need a visa to enter Slovakia.
For Family Members of non-Slovak EU/EEA or Swiss Citizens
If you intend to travel on your own at any time you must apply for a regular visitor/tourist visa, whichever applies and regular visa fee will apply.
Type A – Transit Visa (airport transit visa)
Slovakia does not issue transit visa.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Notice for all visa applicants 
Application for a visa must be submitted in person at the visa centre. All applicants are asked to arrange their appointment in advance. 
Exemptions apply to nationals of some countries which have signed Community-level visa facilitation agreements. Such countries include Albania, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Belarus, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Cape Verde, Montenegro, Macedonia, Moldova, the Russian Federation, Serbia, Georgia, Turkey and Ukraine. With respect to the nationals of the countries, the decision must be delivered within 10 calendar days. Nationals of Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Georgia, Macedonia, Montenegro, Serbia and Ukraine don´t need a visa, if they are holders of biometric passports. 
The submission of complete documentation does not in any way guarantee that your application will be approved. 
In individual cases, where further scrutiny of the application is necessary, the deadline may be extended up to a maximum of 45 calendar days after the application has been considered admissible.
Citizen of Iran, Morocco, Vietnam, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Belarus must submit Slovak Foreign Police verified invitation: Pozvanie cudzinca, Ministerstvo vnútra Slovenskej republiky (https://www.minv.sk/?pozvanie-cudzinca)
The United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland is not a member of the Schengen Area. Citizens of the European Economic Area (EEA) do not need a visa to enter Slovakia.
For Family Members of non-Slovak EU/EEA or Swiss Citizens
If you intend to travel on your own at any time you must apply for a regular visitor/tourist visa, whichever applies and regular visa fee will apply.
Type A – Transit Visa (airport transit visa)
Slovakia does not issue transit visa.

## Visa Fees

[svk > gbr > en > Table:Tourism Visa > Visa Fees 1]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in Cash/QR or by Debit/Credit Card. AmEx not accepted.
VFS Service charge:
Applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 45 Euros (Inclusive VAT 20%) payable in cash or by Debit/Credit Card.
Exemptions apply to:
Citizens of Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia: 35 Euros
Citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Moldova, Georgia, Ukraine: 30 Euros
Please Note:
- The child between 0 - 12 years on the date of submission at VFS are Biometric exempt.
- The applicable visa fee in GBP is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

## Documents Required
Document Checklist – Click here
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries.

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent colour photograph (not more than 6 months old) of yourself, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Please stick the photograph on the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements (click here), then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application centre. 

## Processing Time
From the date of receipt of application at the Embassy of Slovakia, it takes 15 working days to process the visa application. The duration of days when the application is in transit between the Visa Application Centre and the Embassy are not counted. 
In individual cases, where further scrutiny of the application is necessary, the deadline may be extended up to a maximum of 45 calendar days after the application has been considered admissible.

## Download Forms
Please fill your application form in all fields by using the following link:
Slovakia Online Visa Application Form (https://ezov.mzv.sk/e-zov/)
Please make sure:
- Place of Birth: fill with the city you were born
- Applicant’s home address and email address: type full address, including postcode and city, and a valid email address.
- Residence in a country other than the country of current nationality: enter your share code for immigration status
- Entry permit for the final country of destination, where applicable: leave this field blank
Application must be signed with blue-ink pen only

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Business visa application is applicable in case you are travelling for business purposes. This visa is given for travel period of less than 90 days. This visa will be issued for business purpose only; for gainful professional activities (even if the work is not financially rewarded) you have to apply for a work permit.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Business visa application is applicable in case you are travelling for business purposes. This visa is given for travel period of less than 90 days. This visa will be issued for business purpose only; for gainful professional activities (even if the work is not financially rewarded) you have to apply for a work permit.

## Visa Fees

[svk > gbr > en > Table:Business/ Conference > Visa Fees 1]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in Cash/QR or by Debit/Credit Card. AmEx not accepted.
VFS Service charge:
Applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 45 Euros (Inclusive VAT 20%) payable in cash or by Debit/Credit Card.
Exemptions apply to:
Citizens of Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia: 35 Euros
Citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Moldova, Georgia, Ukraine: 30 Euros
Please Note:
- The child between 0 - 12 years on the date of submission at VFS are Biometric exempt.
- The applicable visa fee in GBP is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

## Documents Required
Document Checklist – Click here
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries.

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent colour photograph (not more than 6 months old) of yourself, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Please stick the photograph on the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements (click here), then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application centre. 

## Processing Time
From the date of receipt of application at the Embassy of Slovakia, it takes 15 working days to process the visa application. The duration of days when the application is in transit between the Visa Application Centre and the Embassy are not counted. 
In individual cases, where further scrutiny of the application is necessary, the deadline may be extended up to a maximum of 45 calendar days after the application has been considered admissible.

## Download Forms
Please fill your application form in all fields by using the following link:
Slovakia Online Visa Application Form (https://ezov.mzv.sk/e-zov/)
Please make sure:
- Place of Birth: fill with the city you were born

Application must be signed with blue-ink pen only
